title: lavandula canariensis subsp. fuerteventurae
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. fuerteventurae, also known as the Canary Island lavender, is a subspecies of the Lavandula canariensis plant, native to the Canary Islands, particularly Fuerteventura. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Lavandula canariensis subsp. fuerteventurae include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Lavandula canariensis subsp. fuerteventurae can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
